iso27001-2013.title: Policies for information security
iso27001-2013.description: A set of policies for information security shall be defined, approved by management, published and communicated to employees and relevant external parties.

iso27001-2013.title: Review of the policies for information security
iso27001-2013.description: The policies for information security shall be reviewed at planned intervals or if significant changes occur to ensure their continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.

iso27001-2013.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
iso27001-2013.description: All information security responsibilities shall be defined and allocated.

iso27001-2013.title: Segregation of duties
iso27001-2013.description: Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility shall be segregated to reduce opportunities for unauthorised or unintentional modification or misuse of the organisation's assets.

iso27001-2013.title: Contact with authorities
iso27001-2013.description: Appropriate contacts with relevant authorities shall be maintained.

iso27001-2013.title: Contact with special interest groups
iso27001-2013.description: Appropriate contacts with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations shall be maintained.

iso27001-2013.title: Information security in project management
iso27001-2013.description: Information security shall be addressed in project management, regardless of the type of the project.

iso27001-2013.title: Mobile device policy
iso27001-2013.description: A policy and supporting security measures shall be adopted to manage the risks introduced by using mobile devices.

iso27001-2013.title: Teleworking
iso27001-2013.description: A policy and supporting security measures shall be implemented to protect information accessed, processed or stored at teleworking sites.

iso27001-2013.title: Screening
iso27001-2013.description: Background verification checks on all candidates for employment shall be carried out in accordance with relevant laws, regulations and ethics and shall be proportional to the business requirements, the classification of the information to be accessed and the perceived risks.

iso27001-2013.title: Terms and conditions of employment
iso27001-2013.description: The contractual agreements with employees and contractors shall state their and the organisation's responsibilities for information security.

iso27001-2013.title: Management responsibilities
iso27001-2013.description: Management shall require all employees and contractors to apply information security in accordance with the established policies and procedures of the organisation.

iso27001-2013.title: Information security awareness, education and training
iso27001-2013.description: All employees of the organisation and, where relevant, contractors shall receive appropriate awareness education and training and regular updates in organisational policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

iso27001-2013.title: Disciplinary process
iso27001-2013.description: There shall be a formal and communicated disciplinary process in place to take action against employees who have committed an information security breach.

iso27001-2013.title: Termination or change of employment responsibilities
iso27001-2013.description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, communicated to the employee or contractor and enforced.

iso27001-2013.title: Inventory of assets
iso27001-2013.description: Assets associated with information and information processing facilities shall be identified and an inventory of these assets shall be drawn up and maintained.

iso27001-2013.title: Ownership of assets
iso27001-2013.description: Assets maintained in the inventory shall be owned.

iso27001-2013.title: Acceptable use of assets
iso27001-2013.description: Rules for the acceptable use of information and of assets associated with information and information processing facilities shall be identified, documented and implemented.

iso27001-2013.title: Return of assets
iso27001-2013.description: All employees and external party users shall return all of the organisational assets in their possession upon termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

iso27001-2013.title: Classification of information
iso27001-2013.description: Information shall be classified in terms of legal requirements, value, criticality and sensitivity to unauthorised disclosure or modification.

iso27001-2013.title: Labelling of information
iso27001-2013.description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

iso27001-2013.title: Handling of assets
iso27001-2013.description: Procedures for handling assets shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

iso27001-2013.title: Management of removable media
iso27001-2013.description: Procedures shall be implemented for the management of removable media in accordance with the classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

iso27001-2013.title: Disposal of media
iso27001-2013.description: Media shall be disposed of securely when no longer required, using formal procedures.

iso27001-2013.title: Physical media transfer
iso27001-2013.description: Media containing information shall be protected against unauthorised access, misuse or corruption during transportation.

iso27001-2013.title: Access control policy
iso27001-2013.description: An access control policy shall be established, documented and reviewed based on business and information security requirements.

iso27001-2013.title: Access to networks and network services
iso27001-2013.description: Users shall only be provided with access to the network and network services that they have been specifically authorised to use.

iso27001-2013.title: User registration and de-registration
iso27001-2013.description: A formal user registration and de-registration process shall be implemented to enable assignment of access rights.

iso27001-2013.title: User access provisioning
iso27001-2013.description: A formal user access provisioning process shall be implemented to assign or revoke access rights for all user types to all systems and services.

iso27001-2013.title: Management of privileged access rights
iso27001-2013.description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and controlled.

iso27001-2013.title: Management of secret authentication information of users
iso27001-2013.description: The allocation of secret authentication information shall be controlled through a formal management process.

iso27001-2013.title: Review of user access rights
iso27001-2013.description: Asset owners shall review users' access rights at regular intervals.

iso27001-2013.title: Removal or adjustment of access rights
iso27001-2013.description: The access rights of all employees and external party users to information and information processing facilities shall be removed upon termination of their employment, contract or agreement, or adjusted upon change.

iso27001-2013.title: Use of secret authentication information
iso27001-2013.description: Users shall be required to follow the organisation's practices in the use of secret authentication information.

iso27001-2013.title: Information access restriction
iso27001-2013.description: Access to information and application system functions shall be restricted in accordance with the access control policy.

iso27001-2013.title: Secure log-on procedures
iso27001-2013.description: Where required by the access control policy, access to systems and applications shall be controlled by a secure log-on procedure.

iso27001-2013.title: Password management system
iso27001-2013.description: Password management systems shall be interactive and shall ensure quality passwords.

iso27001-2013.title: Use of privileged utility programs
iso27001-2013.description: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

iso27001-2013.title: Access control to program source code
iso27001-2013.description: Access to program source code shall be restricted.

iso27001-2013.title: Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
iso27001-2013.description: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information shall be developed and implemented.

iso27001-2013.title: Key management
iso27001-2013.description: A policy on the use, protection and lifetime of cryptographic keys shall be developed and implemented through their whole lifecycle.

iso27001-2013.title: Physical security perimeter
iso27001-2013.description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain either sensitive or critical information and information processing facilities.

iso27001-2013.title: Physical entry controls
iso27001-2013.description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls to ensure that only authorised personnel are allowed access.

iso27001-2013.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
iso27001-2013.description: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and applied.

iso27001-2013.title: Protecting against external and environmental threats
iso27001-2013.description: Physical protection against natural disasters, malicious attack or accidents shall be designed and applied.

iso27001-2013.title: Working in secure areas
iso27001-2013.description: Procedures for working in secure areas shall be designed and applied.

iso27001-2013.title: Delivery and loading areas
iso27001-2013.description: Access points such as delivery and loading areas and other points where unauthorised persons could enter the premises shall be controlled and, if possible, isolated from information processing facilities to avoid unauthorised access.

iso27001-2013.title: Equipment siting and protection
iso27001-2013.description: Equipment shall be sited and protected to reduce the risks from environmental threats and hazards, and opportunities for unauthorised access.

iso27001-2013.title: Supporting utilities
iso27001-2013.description: Equipment shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.

iso27001-2013.title: Cabling security
iso27001-2013.description: Power and telecommunications cabling carrying data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference or damage.

iso27001-2013.title: Equipment maintenance
iso27001-2013.description: Equipment shall be correctly maintained to ensure its continued availability and integrity.

iso27001-2013.title: Removal of assets
iso27001-2013.description: Equipment, information or software shall not be taken off-site without prior authorisation.

iso27001-2013.title: Security of equipment and assets off-premises
iso27001-2013.description: Security shall be applied to off-site assets taking into account the different risks of working outside the organisation's premises.

iso27001-2013.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
iso27001-2013.description: All items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

iso27001-2013.title: Unattended user equipment
iso27001-2013.description: Users shall ensure that unattended equipment has appropriate protection.

iso27001-2013.title: Clear desk and clear screen policy
iso27001-2013.description: A clear desk policy for papers and removable storage media and a clear screen policy for information processing facilities shall be adopted.

iso27001-2013.title: Documented operating procedures
iso27001-2013.description: Operating procedures shall be documented and made available to all users who need them.

iso27001-2013.title: Change management
iso27001-2013.description: Changes to the organisation, business processes, information processing facilities and systems that affect information security shall be controlled.

iso27001-2013.title: Capacity management
iso27001-2013.description: The use of resources shall be monitored, tuned and projections made of future capacity requirements to ensure the required system performance.

iso27001-2013.title: Separation of development, testing and operational environments
iso27001-2013.description: Development, testing and operational environments shall be separated to reduce the risks of unauthorised access or changes to the operational environment.

iso27001-2013.title: Controls against malware
iso27001-2013.description: Detection, prevention and recovery controls to protect against malware shall be implemented, combined with appropriate user awareness.

iso27001-2013.title: Information backup
iso27001-2013.description: Backup copies of information, software and system images shall be taken and tested regularly in accordance with an agreed backup policy.

iso27001-2013.title: Event logging
iso27001-2013.description: Event logs recording user activities, exceptions, faults and information security events shall be produced, kept and regularly reviewed.

iso27001-2013.title: Protection of log information
iso27001-2013.description: Logging facilities and log information shall be protected against tampering and unauthorised access.

iso27001-2013.title: Administrator and operator logs
iso27001-2013.description: System administrator and system operator activities shall be logged and the logs protected and regularly reviewed.

iso27001-2013.title: Clock synchronisation
iso27001-2013.description: The clocks of all relevant information processing systems within an organisation or security domain shall be synchronised to a single reference time source.

iso27001-2013.title: Installation of software on operational systems
iso27001-2013.description: Procedures shall be implemented to control the installation of software on operational systems.

iso27001-2013.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
iso27001-2013.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems being used shall be obtained in a timely fashion, the organisation's exposure to such vulnerabilities evaluated and appropriate measures taken to address the associated risk.

iso27001-2013.title: Restrictions on software installation
iso27001-2013.description: Rules governing the installation of software by users shall be established and implemented.

iso27001-2013.title: Information systems audit controls
iso27001-2013.description: Audit requirements and activities involving verification of operational systems shall be carefully planned and agreed to minimise disruptions to business processes.

iso27001-2013.title: Network controls
iso27001-2013.description: Networks shall be managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

iso27001-2013.title: Security of network services
iso27001-2013.description: Security mechanisms, service levels and management requirements of all network services shall be identified and included in network services agreements, whether these services are provided in-house or outsourced.

iso27001-2013.title: Segregation in networks
iso27001-2013.description: Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated on networks.

iso27001-2013.title: Information transfer policies and procedures
iso27001-2013.description: Formal transfer policies, procedures and controls shall be in place to protect the transfer of information through the use of all types of communication facilities.

iso27001-2013.title: Agreements on information transfer
iso27001-2013.description: Agreements shall address the secure transfer of business information between the organisation and external parties.

iso27001-2013.title: Electronic messaging
iso27001-2013.description: Information involved in electronic messaging shall be appropriately protected.

iso27001-2013.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
iso27001-2013.description: Requirements for confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organisation's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, regularly reviewed and documented.

iso27001-2013.title: Information security requirements analysis and specification
iso27001-2013.description: The information security related requirements shall be included in the requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing information systems.

iso27001-2013.title: Securing application services on public networks
iso27001-2013.description: Information involved in application services passing over public networks shall be protected from fraudulent activity, contract dispute and unauthorised disclosure and modification.

iso27001-2013.title: Protecting application services transactions
iso27001-2013.description: Information involved in application service transactions shall be protected to prevent incomplete transmission, mis-routing, unauthorised message alteration, unauthorised disclosure, unauthorised message duplication or replay.

iso27001-2013.title: Secure development policy
iso27001-2013.description: Rules for the development of software and systems shall be established and applied to developments within the organisation.

iso27001-2013.title: System change control procedures
iso27001-2013.description: Changes to systems within the development lifecycle shall be controlled by the use of formal change control procedures.

iso27001-2013.title: Technical review of applications after operating platform changes
iso27001-2013.description: When operating platforms are changed, business critical applications shall be reviewed and tested to ensure there is no adverse impact on organisational operations or security.

iso27001-2013.title: Restrictions on changes to software packages
iso27001-2013.description: Modifications to software packages shall be discouraged, limited to necessary changes and all changes shall be strictly controlled.

iso27001-2013.title: Secure system engineering principles
iso27001-2013.description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system implementation efforts.

iso27001-2013.title: Secure development environment
iso27001-2013.description: Organisations shall establish and appropriately protect secure development environments for system development and integration efforts that cover the entire system development lifecycle.

iso27001-2013.title: Outsourced development
iso27001-2013.description: The organisation shall supervise and monitor the activity of outsourced system development.

iso27001-2013.title: System security testing
iso27001-2013.description: Testing of security functionality shall be carried out during development.

iso27001-2013.title: System acceptance testing
iso27001-2013.description: Acceptance testing programmes and related criteria shall be established for new information systems, upgrades and new versions.

iso27001-2013.title: Protection of test data
iso27001-2013.description: Test data shall be selected carefully, protected and controlled.

iso27001-2013.title: Information security policy for supplier relationships
iso27001-2013.description: Information security requirements for mitigating the risks associated with supplier's access to the organisation's assets shall be agreed with the supplier and documented.

iso27001-2013.title: Addressing security within supplier agreements
iso27001-2013.description: All relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier that may access, process, store, communicate, or provide IT infrastructure components for, the organisation's information.

iso27001-2013.title: Information and communication technology supply chain
iso27001-2013.description: Agreements with suppliers shall include requirements to address the information security risks associated with information and communications technology services and product supply chain.

iso27001-2013.title: Monitoring and review of supplier services
iso27001-2013.description: Organisations shall regularly monitor, review and audit supplier service delivery.

iso27001-2013.title: Managing changes to supplier services
iso27001-2013.description: Changes to the provision of services by suppliers, including maintaining and improving existing information security policies, procedures and controls, shall be managed, taking account of the criticality of business information, systems and processes involved and re-assessment of risks.

iso27001-2013.title: Responsibilities and procedures
iso27001-2013.description: Management responsibilities and procedures shall be established to ensure a quick, effective and orderly response to information security incidents.

iso27001-2013.title: Reporting information security events
iso27001-2013.description: Information security events shall be reported through appropriate management channels as quickly as possible.

iso27001-2013.title: Reporting information security weaknesses
iso27001-2013.description: Employees and contractors using the organisation's information systems and services shall be required to note and report any observed or suspected information security weaknesses in systems or services.

iso27001-2013.title: Assessment of and decision on information security events
iso27001-2013.description: Information security events shall be assessed and it shall be decided if they are to be classified as information security incidents.

iso27001-2013.title: Response to information security incidents
iso27001-2013.description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

iso27001-2013.title: Learning from information security incidents
iso27001-2013.description: Knowledge gained from analysing and resolving information security incidents shall be used to reduce the likelihood or impact of future incidents.

iso27001-2013.title: Collection of evidence
iso27001-2013.description: The organisation shall define and apply procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of information, which can serve as evidence.

iso27001-2013.title: Planning information security continuity
iso27001-2013.description: The organisation shall determine its requirements for information security and the continuity of information security management in adverse situations, e.g. during a crisis or disaster.

iso27001-2013.title: Implementing information security continuity
iso27001-2013.description: The organisation shall establish, document, implement and maintain processes, procedures and controls to ensure the required level of continuity for information security during an adverse situation.

iso27001-2013.title: Verify, review and evaluate information security continuity
iso27001-2013.description: The organisation shall verify the established and implemented information security continuity controls at regular intervals in order to ensure that they are valid and effective during adverse situations.

iso27001-2013.title: Availability of information processing facilities
iso27001-2013.description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

iso27001-2013.title: Identification of applicable legislation and contractual requirements
iso27001-2013.description: All relevant legislative statutory, regulatory, contractual requirements and the organisation's approach to meet these requirements shall be explicitly identified, documented and kept up to date for each information system and the organisation.

iso27001-2013.title: Intellectual property rights
iso27001-2013.description: Appropriate procedures shall be implemented to ensure compliance with legislative, regulatory and contractual requirements related to intellectual property rights and use of proprietary software products.

iso27001-2013.title: Protection of records
iso27001-2013.description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorised access and unauthorised release, in accordance with legislatory, regulatory, contractual and business requirements.

iso27001-2013.title: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information
iso27001-2013.description: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information shall be ensured as required in relevant legislation and regulation where applicable.

iso27001-2013.title: Regulation of cryptographic controls
iso27001-2013.description: Cryptographic controls shall be used in compliance with all relevant agreements, legislation and regulations.

iso27001-2013.title: Independent review of information security
iso27001-2013.description: The organisation's approach to managing information security and its implementation (i.e. control objectives, controls, policies, processes and procedures for information security) shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.

iso27001-2013.title: Compliance with security policies and standards
iso27001-2013.description: Managers shall regularly review the compliance of information processing and procedures within their area of responsibility with the appropriate security policies, standards and any other security requirements.

iso27001-2013.title: Technical compliance review
iso27001-2013.description: Information systems shall be regularly reviewed for compliance with the organisation's information security policies and standards.

iso27001.title: Policies for information security
iso27001.description: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals.

iso27001.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
iso27001.description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organisation needs.

iso27001.title: Segregation of duties
iso27001.description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.

iso27001.title: Management responsibilities
iso27001.description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures of the organisation.

iso27001.title: Contact with authorities
iso27001.description: The organisation shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities.

iso27001.title: Contact with special interest groups
iso27001.description: The organisation shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.

iso27001.title: Threat intelligence
iso27001.description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analysed to produce threat intelligence.

iso27001.title: Information security in project management
iso27001.description: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

iso27001.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
iso27001.description: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained.

iso27001.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
iso27001.description: Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented and implemented.

iso27001.title: Return of assets
iso27001.description: Personnel and other interested parties shall return all the organisation's assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

iso27001.title: Classification of information
iso27001.description: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organisation based on confidentiality, integrity, availability and relevant interested party requirements.

iso27001.title: Labelling of information
iso27001.description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

iso27001.title: Information transfer
iso27001.description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organisation and between the organisation and other parties.

iso27001.title: Access control
iso27001.description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.

iso27001.title: Identity management
iso27001.description: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.

iso27001.title: Authentication information
iso27001.description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information.

iso27001.title: Access rights
iso27001.description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified and removed in accordance with the organisation's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.

iso27001.title: Information security in supplier relationships
iso27001.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services.

iso27001.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
iso27001.description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.

iso27001.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
iso27001.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.

iso27001.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
iso27001.description: The organisation shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.

iso27001.title: Information security for use of cloud services
iso27001.description: Processes for acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organisation's information security requirements.

iso27001.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
iso27001.description: The organisation shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing and communicating information security incident management processes, roles and responsibilities.

iso27001.title: Assessment and decision on information security events
iso27001.description: The organisation shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorised as information security incidents.

iso27001.title: Response to information security incidents
iso27001.description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

iso27001.title: Learning from information security incidents
iso27001.description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.

iso27001.title: Collection of evidence
iso27001.description: The organisation shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events.

iso27001.title: Information security during disruption
iso27001.description: The organisation shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.

iso27001.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
iso27001.description: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.

iso27001.title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
iso27001.description: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organisation's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented and kept up to date.

iso27001.title: Intellectual property rights
iso27001.description: The organisation shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.

iso27001.title: Protection of records
iso27001.description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorised access and unauthorised release.

iso27001.title: Privacy and protection of personal identifiable information
iso27001.description: The organisation shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of personal identifiable information according to applicable laws and regulations.

iso27001.title: Independent review of information security
iso27001.description: The organisation's approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.

iso27001.title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
iso27001.description: Compliance with the organisation's information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed.

iso27001.title: Documented operating procedures
iso27001.description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.

iso27001.title: Screening
iso27001.description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organisation and on an ongoing basis taking into consideration applicable laws, regulations and ethics.

iso27001.title: Terms and conditions of employment
iso27001.description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organisation's responsibilities for information security.

iso27001.title: Information security awareness, education and training
iso27001.description: Personnel of the organisation and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training and regular updates of the organisation's information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

iso27001.title: Disciplinary process
iso27001.description: A disciplinary process shall be formalised and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation.

iso27001.title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
iso27001.description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.

iso27001.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
iso27001.description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organisation's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.

iso27001.title: Remote working
iso27001.description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed or stored outside the organisation's premises.

iso27001.title: Information security event reporting
iso27001.description: The organisation shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.

iso27001.title: Physical security perimeters
iso27001.description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

iso27001.title: Physical entry
iso27001.description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.

iso27001.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
iso27001.description: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented.

iso27001.title: Physical security monitoring
iso27001.description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorised physical access.

iso27001.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
iso27001.description: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.

iso27001.title: Working in secure areas
iso27001.description: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.

iso27001.title: Clear desk and clear screen
iso27001.description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.

iso27001.title: Equipment siting and protection
iso27001.description: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected.

iso27001.title: Security of assets off-premises
iso27001.description: Off-site assets shall be protected.

iso27001.title: Storage media
iso27001.description: Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition, use, transportation and disposal in accordance with the organisation's classification scheme and handling requirements.

iso27001.title: Supporting utilities
iso27001.description: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.

iso27001.title: Cabling security
iso27001.description: Cables carrying power, data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference or damage.

iso27001.title: Equipment maintenance
iso27001.description: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity and confidentiality of information.

iso27001.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
iso27001.description: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

iso27001.title: User endpoint devices
iso27001.description: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

iso27001.title: Privileged access rights
iso27001.description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

iso27001.title: Information access restriction
iso27001.description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.

iso27001.title: Access to source code
iso27001.description: Read and write access to source code, development tools and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.

iso27001.title: Secure authentication
iso27001.description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.

iso27001.title: Capacity management
iso27001.description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.

iso27001.title: Protection against malware
iso27001.description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.

iso27001.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
iso27001.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained in a timely fashion, the organisation's exposure to such vulnerabilities evaluated and appropriate measures taken.

iso27001.title: Configuration management
iso27001.description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.

iso27001.title: Information deletion
iso27001.description: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.

iso27001.title: Data masking
iso27001.description: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organisation's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration.

iso27001.title: Data leakage prevention
iso27001.description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.

iso27001.title: Information backup
iso27001.description: Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.

iso27001.title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
iso27001.description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

iso27001.title: Logging
iso27001.description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.

iso27001.title: Monitoring activities
iso27001.description: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

iso27001.title: Clock synchronisation
iso27001.description: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organisation shall be synchronised to approved time sources.

iso27001.title: Use of privileged utility programs
iso27001.description: The use of utility programs that can be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

iso27001.title: Installation of software on operational systems
iso27001.description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems.

iso27001.title: Networks security
iso27001.description: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

iso27001.title: Security of network services
iso27001.description: Security mechanisms, service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.

iso27001.title: Segregation of networks
iso27001.description: Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated in the organisation's networks.

iso27001.title: Web filtering
iso27001.description: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

iso27001.title: Use of cryptography
iso27001.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

iso27001.title: Secure development life cycle
iso27001.description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

iso27001.title: Application security requirements
iso27001.description: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.

iso27001.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
iso27001.description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system development or integration activities.

iso27001.title: Secure coding
iso27001.description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

iso27001.title: Security testing in development and acceptance
iso27001.description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.

iso27001.title: Outsourced development
iso27001.description: The organisation shall direct, monitor and review the activities related to outsourced system development.

iso27001.title: Separation of development, test and production environments
iso27001.description: Development, testing and production environments shall be separated and secured.

iso27001.title: Change management
iso27001.description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.

iso27001.title: Test information
iso27001.description: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed.

iso27001.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
iso27001.description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.

nis2.title: Policies on risk analysis and information system security
nis2.description: Essential and important entities shall implement cybersecurity risk-management measures encompassing policies on risk analysis and information system security.

nis2.title: Incident handling
nis2.description: Measures for incident handling including incident prevention, detection, and response, as well as the recovery from incidents.

nis2.title: Business continuity and crisis management
nis2.description: Business continuity, such as backup management and disaster recovery, and crisis management.

nis2.title: Supply chain security
nis2.description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

nis2.title: Security in network and information systems acquisition
nis2.description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

nis2.title: Policies and procedures to assess effectiveness of cybersecurity measures
nis2.description: Policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures.

nis2.title: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training
nis2.description: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training.

nis2.title: Policies and procedures regarding cryptography
nis2.description: Policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography and, where appropriate, encryption.

nis2.title: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management
nis2.description: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management.

nis2.title: Use of multi-factor authentication
nis2.description: Use of multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions, secured voice, video and text communications and secured emergency communication systems.

nis2.title: Incident notification obligations
nis2.description: Essential and important entities shall notify the CSIRT or competent authority of any significant incident within specific timeframes.

nis2.title: Early warning and incident notification
nis2.description: Early warning within 24 hours, notification within 72 hours, and final report within one month of becoming aware of a significant incident.

nis2.title: Governance — management body responsibility
nis2.description: Member States shall ensure that the management bodies of essential and important entities approve the cybersecurity risk-management measures and oversee implementation.

nis2.title: Governance — management body training
nis2.description: Management body members shall follow training and encourage personnel to acquire sufficient cybersecurity knowledge and skills on a regular basis.

gdpr.title: Integrity and confidentiality
gdpr.description: Personal data shall be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures.

gdpr.title: Data protection by design and by default
gdpr.description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed.

gdpr.title: Records of processing activities
gdpr.description: Each controller shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility.

gdpr.title: Security of processing
gdpr.description: The controller and processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including pseudonymisation, encryption, ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems.

gdpr.title: Notification of personal data breach to supervisory authority
gdpr.description: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall notify the competent supervisory authority within 72 hours of having become aware of it.

gdpr.title: Communication of personal data breach to data subject
gdpr.description: When a personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the breach to the data subject without undue delay.

gdpr.title: Data protection impact assessment
gdpr.description: The controller shall carry out a data protection impact assessment where processing is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.

gdpr.title: Designation of the data protection officer
gdpr.description: The controller and processor shall designate a data protection officer where the core activities consist of processing operations which require regular and systematic monitoring of data subjects.

gdpr.title: Processor
gdpr.description: Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller, the controller shall use only processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures.

dora.title: ICT risk management framework
dora.description: Financial entities shall have in place a sound, comprehensive and well-documented ICT risk management framework as part of their overall risk management system.

dora.title: ICT risk management — systems and tools
dora.description: Financial entities shall use and maintain updated ICT systems, protocols and tools that are appropriate, reliable and sufficient to address the ICT risk.

dora.title: ICT asset management
dora.description: Financial entities shall identify, classify and document all ICT supported business functions and the ICT assets supporting them.

dora.title: ICT risk identification
dora.description: Financial entities shall identify and classify ICT-related incidents, continuously perform risk and vulnerability assessments with respect to all ICT systems.

dora.title: Protection and prevention
dora.description: Financial entities shall put in place appropriate ICT security policies, procedures, protocols and tools to protect ICT systems and ICT-enabled business processes.

dora.title: Detection
dora.description: Financial entities shall have in place mechanisms to promptly detect anomalous activities including ICT network performance issues and ICT-related incidents.

dora.title: Response and recovery
dora.description: Financial entities shall have in place a comprehensive ICT business continuity policy as part of their sound business continuity management system.

dora.title: Backup policies and restoration
dora.description: Financial entities shall have backup policies and procedures, backup and restoration methods, to ensure that data and ICT systems can be restored.

dora.title: Learning and evolving
dora.description: Financial entities shall gather information on vulnerabilities and cyber threats and conduct post-ICT-related incident reviews after significant disruptions.

dora.title: ICT-related incident management process
dora.description: Financial entities shall define, establish and implement an ICT-related incident management process to detect, manage and notify ICT-related incidents.

dora.title: Reporting of major ICT-related incidents
dora.description: Financial entities shall report major ICT-related incidents to the relevant competent authority within timeframes specified in the regulation.

dora.title: General requirements for digital operational resilience testing
dora.description: Financial entities shall establish, maintain and review a sound and comprehensive digital operational resilience testing programme.

dora.title: ICT third-party risk management
dora.description: Financial entities shall manage ICT third-party risk as an integral component of ICT risk within their ICT risk management framework.

dora.title: Key contractual provisions for ICT third-party service providers
dora.description: Rights and obligations of the financial entity and of the ICT third-party service provider shall be clearly allocated and set out in a written arrangement.

cis.title: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
cis.description: Actively manage all enterprise assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments to accurately know the totality of assets that need to be monitored and protected.

cis.title: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
cis.description: Actively manage all software on the network so that only authorised software is installed and can execute, and that unauthorised and unmanaged software is found and prevented from installation or execution.

cis.title: Data Protection
cis.description: Develop processes and technical controls to identify, classify, securely handle, retain, and dispose of data.

cis.title: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
cis.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of enterprise assets and software.

cis.title: Account Management
cis.description: Use processes and tools to assign and manage authorisation to credentials for user accounts, including administrator accounts, as well as service accounts, to enterprise assets and software.

cis.title: Access Control Management
cis.description: Use processes and tools to create, assign, manage, and revoke access credentials and privileges for user, administrator, and service accounts for enterprise assets and software.

cis.title: Continuous Vulnerability Management
cis.description: Develop a plan to continuously assess and track vulnerabilities on all enterprise assets within the infrastructure in order to remediate and minimise the window of opportunity for attackers.

cis.title: Audit Log Management
cis.description: Collect, alert, review, and retain audit logs of events that could help detect, understand, or recover from an attack.

cis.title: Email and Web Browser Protections
cis.description: Improve protections and detections of threats from email and web vectors, as these are opportunities for attackers to manipulate human behaviour.

cis.title: Malware Defences
cis.description: Prevent or control the installation, spread, and execution of malicious applications, code, or scripts on enterprise assets.

cis.title: Data Recovery
cis.description: Establish and maintain data recovery practices sufficient to restore in-scope enterprise assets to a pre-incident and trusted state.

cis.title: Network Infrastructure Management
cis.description: Establish, implement, and actively manage network devices, in order to prevent attackers from exploiting vulnerable network services and access points.

cis.title: Network Monitoring and Defence
cis.description: Operate processes and tooling to establish and maintain comprehensive network monitoring and defence against security threats.

cis.title: Security Awareness and Skills Training
cis.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness programme to influence behaviour among the workforce to be security conscious and properly skilled.

cis.title: Service Provider Management
cis.description: Develop a process to evaluate service providers who hold sensitive data or are responsible for an enterprise's critical IT platforms or processes.

cis.title: Application Software Security
cis.description: Manage the security life cycle of in-house developed, hosted, or acquired software to prevent, detect, and remediate security weaknesses.

cis.title: Incident Response Management
cis.description: Establish a programme to develop and maintain an incident response capability in order to prepare, detect, contain, and eradicate attacks.

cis.title: Penetration Testing
cis.description: Test the effectiveness and resiliency of enterprise assets through identifying and exploiting weaknesses in controls, and simulating the objectives and actions of an attacker.

nistcsf.title: Organisational Context
nistcsf.description: The circumstances surrounding the organisation's cybersecurity risk management decisions are understood.

nistcsf.title: Risk Management Strategy
nistcsf.description: The organisation's priorities, constraints, risk tolerance and appetite statements, and assumptions are established, communicated, and used to support operational risk decisions.

nistcsf.title: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
nistcsf.description: Cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities to foster accountability, performance assessment, and continuous improvement are established and communicated.

nistcsf.title: Policy
nistcsf.description: Organisational cybersecurity policy is established, communicated, and enforced.

nistcsf.title: Oversight
nistcsf.description: Results of organisation-wide cybersecurity risk management activities and performance are used to inform, improve, and adjust the risk management strategy.

nistcsf.title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
nistcsf.description: Cyber supply chain risk management processes are identified, established, managed, monitored, and improved by organisational stakeholders.

nistcsf.title: Asset Management
nistcsf.description: Assets that enable the organisation to achieve business purposes are identified and managed consistent with their relative importance to organisational objectives and the organisation's risk strategy.

nistcsf.title: Risk Assessment
nistcsf.description: The cybersecurity risk to the organisation, assets, and individuals is understood by the organisation.

nistcsf.title: Improvement
nistcsf.description: Improvements to organisational cybersecurity risk management processes, procedures and activities are identified across all CSF Functions.

nistcsf.title: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
nistcsf.description: Access to physical and logical assets is limited to authorised users, services, and hardware and managed commensurate with the assessed risk of unauthorised access.

nistcsf.title: Awareness and Training
nistcsf.description: The organisation's personnel are provided with cybersecurity awareness and training so that they can perform their cybersecurity-related tasks.

nistcsf.title: Data Security
nistcsf.description: Data are managed consistent with the organisation's risk strategy to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information.

nistcsf.title: Platform Security
nistcsf.description: The hardware, software, and services of physical and virtual platforms are managed consistent with the organisation's risk strategy to protect their confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

nistcsf.title: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
nistcsf.description: Security architectures are managed with the organisation's risk strategy to protect asset confidentiality, integrity, and availability and organisational resilience.

nistcsf.title: Continuous Monitoring
nistcsf.description: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events.

nistcsf.title: Adverse Event Analysis
nistcsf.description: Anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events are analysed to characterise the events and detect cybersecurity incidents.

nistcsf.title: Incident Management
nistcsf.description: Responses to detected cybersecurity incidents are managed.

nistcsf.title: Incident Analysis
nistcsf.description: Investigations are conducted to ensure effective response and support forensics and recovery activities.

nistcsf.title: Incident Response Reporting and Communication
nistcsf.description: Response activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders as required by laws, regulations, or policies.

nistcsf.title: Incident Mitigation
nistcsf.description: Activities are performed to prevent expansion of an event and mitigate its effects.

nistcsf.title: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
nistcsf.description: Restoration activities are performed to ensure operational availability of systems and services affected by cybersecurity incidents.

nistcsf.title: Incident Recovery Communication
nistcsf.description: Restoration activities are coordinated with internal and external parties.

kritis.title: State-of-the-art technical and organisational security measures
kritis.description: Operators of critical infrastructure must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to avoid disruptions of availability, integrity, authenticity and confidentiality of their IT systems, components and processes that are essential to the functioning of the critical infrastructure they operate. The measures must comply with the state of the art.

kritis.title: Systems for attack detection
kritis.description: Operators of critical infrastructure must use systems for attack detection (Angriffserkennung). These systems must continuously analyse incoming and outgoing data traffic and system states in order to detect attacks and enable appropriate defensive and corrective actions.

kritis.title: Proof of compliance every two years
kritis.description: Operators of critical infrastructure must demonstrate to BSI, every two years, that the security measures required under §8a(1) and §8a(1a) have been implemented. Acceptable forms of proof include security audits, examinations, or certifications. BSI may request supplementary documentation or carry out its own investigation.

kritis.title: Registration with BSI
kritis.description: Operators of critical infrastructure must register with BSI and designate a contact point that is reachable at all times. The contact point ensures the exchange of information between the operator and BSI and must be reported without undue delay.

kritis.title: Incident reporting to BSI
kritis.description: Operators of critical infrastructure must report to BSI without undue delay any significant disruptions to the availability, integrity, authenticity or confidentiality of their IT systems that result in, or could result in, a failure or significant impairment of the critical infrastructure they operate.

kritis.title: Critical components and manufacturers
kritis.description: Components critical to the functioning of critical infrastructure (kritische Komponenten) may only be used after the operator has confirmed that the manufacturer has issued a declaration of trustworthiness. The Federal Ministry of the Interior may prohibit the use of critical components from manufacturers that do not provide sufficient trustworthiness guarantees.

kritis.title: Industry-specific security standards as compliance reference
kritis.description: Operators of critical infrastructure may demonstrate compliance with §8a(1) by implementing security standards developed by industry associations and recognised by BSI (Branchenspezifische Sicherheitsstandards, B3S). BSI may reject such standards or request amendments if they do not adequately reflect the state of the art.

kritis.title: BSI audit and inspection powers
kritis.description: BSI may at any time request documentation and evidence from critical infrastructure operators regarding the implementation of required security measures, or commission an independent third party to carry out an audit. BSI may also issue binding orders to remedy identified deficiencies within a set timeframe.

kritis.title: Contact point always reachable
kritis.description: Operators of critical infrastructure must designate a permanent, always-reachable contact point (Kontaktstelle) for communication with BSI. This contact point must be reported to BSI and updated without undue delay in the event of any change. The contact point serves as the primary interface for BSI communications, warnings, and incident coordination.

kritis.title: Obligations for digital infrastructure operators
kritis.description: Operators of digital infrastructure (including internet exchange points, DNS service providers, TLD name registries, and cloud computing services) are subject to analogous security obligations as critical infrastructure operators. They must implement state-of-the-art measures, prove compliance, maintain a reachable contact point, and report significant security incidents to BSI.

kritis.title: Security obligations for entities of special public interest (UNBÖFI)
kritis.description: Entities of special public interest (Unternehmen im besonderen öffentlichen Interesse) — such as defence contractors, entities with significant economic importance, and providers of particularly important services — must submit self-declarations of implemented security measures to BSI, report significant incidents, and register with BSI, even if they do not qualify as critical infrastructure under the numerical thresholds.

nis2-umsg.title: Risk management and minimum security measures for essential entities
nis2-umsg.description: Essential entities must take appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to manage risks posed to the security of their network and information systems. Measures must address policies on risk analysis and information system security, incident handling, business continuity, supply chain security, security in acquisition and maintenance, effectiveness assessment, cyber hygiene and training, cryptography, human resources security and access control, and multi-factor authentication.

nis2-umsg.title: Minimum security measures for important entities
nis2-umsg.description: Important entities must implement the same minimum security measures as essential entities under §30, with the difference that supervisory obligations are triggered only after an incident or relevant indication, rather than on a proactive basis.

nis2-umsg.title: Incident notification obligations
nis2-umsg.description: Essential and important entities must notify BSI without undue delay of any significant incident affecting their network and information systems. An early warning must be submitted within 24 hours, a detailed incident notification within 72 hours, and a final report within one month of the initial notification.

nis2-umsg.title: Management body responsibility and liability
nis2-umsg.description: The management bodies of essential and important entities must approve the cybersecurity risk management measures, oversee their implementation, and can be held personally liable for breaches of these obligations. Management must undergo regular cybersecurity training to acquire sufficient knowledge and skills to identify and assess cybersecurity risks.

nis2-umsg.title: Supply chain security
nis2-umsg.description: Essential and important entities must address cybersecurity risks in their supply chains and supplier relationships, including security aspects of the relationships between each entity and its direct suppliers and service providers, and evaluate the overall security practices of the supply chain.

nis2-umsg.title: Registration and information obligations
nis2-umsg.description: Essential and important entities must register with BSI and provide relevant information including name, address, sector, contact details, and IP address ranges. Updates must be notified to BSI without undue delay. BSI maintains a register of essential and important entities.

nis2-umsg.title: Classification of essential and important entities
nis2-umsg.description: Entities are classified based on their sector and size. Essential entities: large entities (>250 employees or >€50M annual turnover) in Annex I sectors (energy, transport, banking, financial market infrastructure, health, drinking water, wastewater, digital infrastructure, ICT service management, public administration, space). Important entities: medium entities (>50 employees or >€10M turnover) in Annex I sectors, and large and medium entities in Annex II sectors (postal services, waste management, chemicals, food, manufacturing, digital providers, research). Certain entities are classified as essential regardless of size, including providers of public electronic communications networks, trust service providers, DNS service providers, and TLD registries.

nis2-umsg.title: Minimum security measures — 12 categories
nis2-umsg.description: Essential and important entities must implement measures across twelve minimum security categories: (1) risk analysis and information system security policies; (2) incident handling; (3) business continuity including backup management and disaster recovery; (4) supply chain security; (5) security in acquisition, development and maintenance of network and information systems; (6) policies to assess effectiveness of cybersecurity measures; (7) basic cyber hygiene and cybersecurity training; (8) cryptography and encryption policies; (9) human resources security, access control and asset management; (10) multi-factor or continuous authentication; (11) secure communications; (12) emergency communications systems.

nis2-umsg.title: Supervisory and enforcement measures for essential entities
nis2-umsg.description: Essential entities are subject to proactive, ex-ante supervision. BSI and sector-specific authorities may conduct on-site inspections, targeted security audits, scans of publicly accessible systems, and requests for evidence of compliance. BSI may issue binding corrective orders, impose operational restrictions, or temporarily prohibit operation in cases of significant non-compliance that poses an unacceptable risk.

nis2-umsg.title: Voluntary incident reporting
nis2-umsg.description: Entities not classified as essential or important, and classified entities reporting incidents below the significance threshold, may voluntarily notify BSI of incidents, cyber threats, and near-misses. BSI processes voluntary reports, may publish anonymised threat intelligence derived from them, and provides feedback to reporting entities. Voluntary reporting does not attract administrative sanctions.

nis2-umsg.title: Use of European cybersecurity certification
nis2-umsg.description: BSI may require essential and important entities to use products, services and processes certified under European cybersecurity certification schemes established pursuant to the EU Cybersecurity Act (Regulation 2019/881). Where a relevant scheme exists and the entity uses uncertified products, it must justify this to the supervisory authority. BSI operates Germany's national certification authority (Nationale Zertifizierungsstelle, NZS) for this purpose.

nis2-umsg.title: Information sharing and sector cooperation
nis2-umsg.description: Essential and important entities are encouraged to participate in information sharing arrangements (ISACs or equivalent bodies) relevant to their sector. BSI may establish or designate such bodies and coordinates threat intelligence sharing with ENISA and EU member states. Entities that share cybersecurity information in good faith are granted liability protection for that disclosure.

nis2-umsg.title: Administrative fines
nis2-umsg.description: Violations of the security and reporting obligations may result in administrative fines of up to €10,000,000 or 2% of total worldwide annual turnover for essential entities, and €7,000,000 or 1.4% of turnover for important entities, whichever is higher. Management body members can be held personally liable and may face temporary bans from management functions for wilful or seriously negligent failures.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Security Management
bsi-grundschutz.description: The ISMS.1 building block covers the establishment, operation and continuous improvement of an information security management process. It requires a defined security policy, assigned responsibilities, documented security concept, and regular review of the overall ISMS.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Organisation
bsi-grundschutz.description: The ORP.1 building block requires that information security responsibilities are clearly defined and allocated within the organisation. It covers the organisational structure, role definitions, and the processes for managing security-relevant decisions.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Personnel
bsi-grundschutz.description: The ORP.2 building block addresses the security aspects of employment, covering background checks for sensitive roles, contractual obligations, responsibilities upon change or termination of employment, and measures to protect against insider threats.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Information Security Awareness and Training
bsi-grundschutz.description: The ORP.3 building block requires that all employees receive regular and role-appropriate security awareness and training. It covers the development of a training programme, awareness campaigns, and measuring the effectiveness of security education.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Identity and Access Management
bsi-grundschutz.description: The ORP.4 building block governs the management of user identities and access rights across all systems. It requires a defined process for granting, reviewing and revoking access rights, the use of the principle of least privilege, and the use of strong authentication methods.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Cryptography Concept
bsi-grundschutz.description: The CON.1 building block requires the establishment of a cryptography policy covering the selection of approved algorithms and key lengths, key management processes, and the use of encryption to protect the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive information in storage and transit.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Data Backup Policy
bsi-grundschutz.description: The CON.3 building block requires that a documented data backup policy is established and maintained. It covers backup frequency, retention periods, backup storage locations, testing of restoration procedures, and protection of backup media against loss, destruction and unauthorised access.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Patch and Change Management
bsi-grundschutz.description: The OPS.1.1.3 building block requires a structured process for managing software patches and configuration changes. It covers vulnerability scanning, patch prioritisation, testing changes in non-production environments, and maintaining a record of applied patches and configuration changes.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Detection of Relevant Security Events
bsi-grundschutz.description: The DER.1 building block requires the establishment of monitoring and detection capabilities to identify security-relevant events in a timely manner. It covers logging requirements, log analysis, use of security information and event management (SIEM) tools, and detection of anomalies and attack patterns.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Handling IT Security Incidents
bsi-grundschutz.description: The DER.2.1 building block defines the requirements for detecting, reporting, classifying, responding to and documenting IT security incidents. It requires defined roles, documented response procedures, post-incident analysis to prevent recurrence, and communication with relevant internal and external stakeholders.

bsi-grundschutz.title: IT Emergency Management (BCM)
bsi-grundschutz.description: The DER.4 building block requires the development and maintenance of IT emergency management plans as part of the overall business continuity management (BCM) process. It covers business impact analysis, recovery time objectives, emergency procedures, testing of continuity plans, and crisis communication.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Network Architecture and Design
bsi-grundschutz.description: The NET.1.1 building block requires that network architecture is designed with security as a foundational principle. It covers network segmentation, the use of demilitarised zones, secure remote access, protection of management interfaces, and documentation of the network topology.

bsi-grundschutz.title: General Server
bsi-grundschutz.description: The SYS.1.1 building block defines baseline security requirements for all server systems, including hardening of the operating system, removal of unnecessary services and accounts, protection of administrative interfaces, regular patching, and monitoring of server health and security events.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Requirements Management (Compliance)
bsi-grundschutz.description: The ORP.5 building block requires identification and ongoing management of all legal, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security. It covers establishing a compliance management process, assigning responsibilities for regulatory monitoring, maintaining a register of applicable obligations, and ensuring that the organisation's security measures satisfy those obligations.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Privacy (Datenschutz)
bsi-grundschutz.description: The CON.2 building block addresses data protection requirements integrated within the ISMS. It requires that personal data is processed only to the extent necessary, that data protection impact assessments are conducted for high-risk processing activities, that a Data Protection Officer is designated where required by GDPR, and that appropriate technical and organisational measures protect the rights of data subjects.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Delete and Destroy
bsi-grundschutz.description: The CON.6 building block requires that information and data are securely deleted or destroyed when they are no longer required. It covers the development of a data retention and deletion policy, selection of appropriate deletion methods proportionate to data classification, secure disposal and destruction of storage media, verification of deletion effectiveness, and documentation of destruction for sensitive data.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Proper IT Operations
bsi-grundschutz.description: The OPS.1.1.2 building block defines requirements for the day-to-day operation of IT systems, covering documented and approved operating procedures, separation of production and test environments, capacity planning to prevent performance degradation, regular review of system configurations against approved baselines, and structured management of scheduled and emergency maintenance to ensure availability and integrity.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Protection Against Malware
bsi-grundschutz.description: The OPS.1.1.4 building block defines requirements for protecting IT systems against malware.
