It covers deployment of up-to-date anti-malware solutions on all relevant endpoints and servers, automatic and regular signature and behavioural-detection updates, real-time scanning, scanning of incoming files and email attachments, centrally managed malware policies, and user awareness training to prevent malware infections.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Logging
bsi-grundschutz.description: The OPS.1.1.5 building block defines requirements for generating, securely storing, protecting, and analysing log data. It covers defining a logging concept specifying which systems and events must be logged, protecting log files against tampering and unauthorised access, defining and enforcing retention periods, archiving log data, and regularly reviewing logs for security-relevant events and anomalies.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Cloud Usage
bsi-grundschutz.description: The OPS.2.2 building block governs the secure use of cloud services. It covers classification of data before migration to cloud environments, contractual security obligations binding the cloud service provider, encryption of data in transit and at rest, identity and access management for cloud resources, continuous monitoring of cloud service security posture, and documented exit strategies for transitioning away from a cloud service.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Outsourcing to Third Parties
bsi-grundschutz.description: The OPS.2.3 building block addresses security requirements when IT services or processes are outsourced. It requires risk assessment prior to outsourcing decisions, security requirements embedded in contracts including rights of audit, security obligations extended to subcontractors, continuous monitoring and review of outsourced service security levels, and documented exit strategies to ensure business continuity when transitioning services.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Audits and Revisions
bsi-grundschutz.description: The DER.3.1 building block defines requirements for conducting regular information security audits and revisions to verify that security measures are implemented correctly and remain effective. It covers establishing an audit programme, defining scope and criteria, conducting internal and external audits, documenting findings, tracking remediation of deficiencies, and reporting audit results to management to support continuous improvement.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Firewall
bsi-grundschutz.description: The NET.3.2 building block defines requirements for firewall systems that control and filter network traffic between security zones. It covers defining a firewall policy with a deny-all-by-default rule set, secure configuration and hardening of firewall systems, documented change management for rule modifications, regular review and optimisation of rule sets, logging of connection attempts and denied traffic, and periodic testing of firewall effectiveness.

bsi-grundschutz.title: General Buildings
bsi-grundschutz.description: The INF.1 building block addresses physical security requirements for buildings that house IT systems or process sensitive information. It covers physical security perimeters, access control for building zones, protection against environmental threats such as fire, flood, extreme temperatures and power failure, visitor management, physical security of cable infrastructure, and security requirements for building construction and renovation.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Data Centre and Server Room
bsi-grundschutz.description: The INF.2 building block defines specific physical security requirements for data centres and server rooms. It covers access controls restricted to authorised personnel only, environmental monitoring including temperature, humidity, smoke and water ingress detection, redundant and conditioned power supply and cooling, fire detection and suppression systems, physical layout minimising risk of collateral damage, and regular testing of all protective systems.

bsi-grundschutz.title: General Client
bsi-grundschutz.description: The SYS.2.1 building block defines baseline security requirements for all client systems including workstations and laptops. It covers operating system hardening and removal of unnecessary services and software, local firewall configuration, automatic screen lock and session timeouts, encrypted storage for sensitive data, endpoint protection including anti-malware and host-based controls, and restrictions on local administrator rights.

bsi-grundschutz.title: Web Applications and Web Services
bsi-grundschutz.description: The APP.3.1 building block defines security requirements for web applications and web services. It covers secure design and architecture principles, input validation and output encoding to prevent injection attacks, robust authentication and session management, protection against common web vulnerabilities (OWASP Top 10), secure error handling that avoids information leakage, and regular security testing including vulnerability scanning and penetration testing.

iso27002.title: Policies for information security
iso27002.description: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals.

iso27002.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
iso27002.description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organisation needs.

iso27002.title: Segregation of duties
iso27002.description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.

iso27002.title: Management responsibilities
iso27002.description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures of the organisation.

iso27002.title: Contact with authorities
iso27002.description: The organisation shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities.

iso27002.title: Contact with special interest groups
iso27002.description: The organisation shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.

iso27002.title: Threat intelligence
iso27002.description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analysed to produce threat intelligence.

iso27002.title: Information security in project management
iso27002.description: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

iso27002.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
iso27002.description: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained.

iso27002.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
iso27002.description: Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented and implemented.

iso27002.title: Return of assets
iso27002.description: Personnel and other interested parties shall return all the organisation's assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

iso27002.title: Classification of information
iso27002.description: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organisation based on confidentiality, integrity, availability and relevant interested party requirements.

iso27002.title: Labelling of information
iso27002.description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

iso27002.title: Information transfer
iso27002.description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organisation and between the organisation and other parties.

iso27002.title: Access control
iso27002.description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.

iso27002.title: Identity management
iso27002.description: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.

iso27002.title: Authentication information
iso27002.description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information.

iso27002.title: Access rights
iso27002.description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified and removed in accordance with the organisation's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.

iso27002.title: Information security in supplier relationships
iso27002.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services.

iso27002.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
iso27002.description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.

iso27002.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
iso27002.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.

iso27002.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
iso27002.description: The organisation shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.

iso27002.title: Information security for use of cloud services
iso27002.description: Processes for acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organisation's information security requirements.

iso27002.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
iso27002.description: The organisation shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing and communicating information security incident management processes, roles and responsibilities.

iso27002.title: Assessment and decision on information security events
iso27002.description: The organisation shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorised as information security incidents.

iso27002.title: Response to information security incidents
iso27002.description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

iso27002.title: Learning from information security incidents
iso27002.description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.

iso27002.title: Collection of evidence
iso27002.description: The organisation shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events.

iso27002.title: Information security during disruption
iso27002.description: The organisation shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.

iso27002.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
iso27002.description: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.

iso27002.title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
iso27002.description: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organisation's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented and kept up to date.

iso27002.title: Intellectual property rights
iso27002.description: The organisation shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.

iso27002.title: Protection of records
iso27002.description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorised access and unauthorised release.

iso27002.title: Privacy and protection of personal identifiable information
iso27002.description: The organisation shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of personal identifiable information according to applicable laws and regulations.

iso27002.title: Independent review of information security
iso27002.description: The organisation's approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.

iso27002.title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
iso27002.description: Compliance with the organisation's information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed.

iso27002.title: Documented operating procedures
iso27002.description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.

iso27002.title: Screening
iso27002.description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organisation and on an ongoing basis taking into consideration applicable laws, regulations and ethics.

iso27002.title: Terms and conditions of employment
iso27002.description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organisation's responsibilities for information security.

iso27002.title: Information security awareness, education and training
iso27002.description: Personnel of the organisation and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training and regular updates of the organisation's information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

iso27002.title: Disciplinary process
iso27002.description: A disciplinary process shall be formalised and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation.

iso27002.title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
iso27002.description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.

iso27002.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
iso27002.description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organisation's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.

iso27002.title: Remote working
iso27002.description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed or stored outside the organisation's premises.

iso27002.title: Information security event reporting
iso27002.description: The organisation shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.

iso27002.title: Physical security perimeters
iso27002.description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

iso27002.title: Physical entry
iso27002.description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.

iso27002.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
iso27002.description: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented.

iso27002.title: Physical security monitoring
iso27002.description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorised physical access.

iso27002.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
iso27002.description: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.

iso27002.title: Working in secure areas
iso27002.description: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.

iso27002.title: Clear desk and clear screen
iso27002.description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.

iso27002.title: Equipment siting and protection
iso27002.description: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected.

iso27002.title: Security of assets off-premises
iso27002.description: Off-site assets shall be protected.

iso27002.title: Storage media
iso27002.description: Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition, use, transportation and disposal in accordance with the organisation's classification scheme and handling requirements.

iso27002.title: Supporting utilities
iso27002.description: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.

iso27002.title: Cabling security
iso27002.description: Cables carrying power, data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference or damage.

iso27002.title: Equipment maintenance
iso27002.description: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity and confidentiality of information.

iso27002.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
iso27002.description: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

iso27002.title: User endpoint devices
iso27002.description: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

iso27002.title: Privileged access rights
iso27002.description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

iso27002.title: Information access restriction
iso27002.description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.

iso27002.title: Access to source code
iso27002.description: Read and write access to source code, development tools and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.

iso27002.title: Secure authentication
iso27002.description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.

iso27002.title: Capacity management
iso27002.description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.

iso27002.title: Protection against malware
iso27002.description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.

iso27002.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
iso27002.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained in a timely fashion, the organisation's exposure to such vulnerabilities evaluated and appropriate measures taken.

iso27002.title: Configuration management
iso27002.description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.

iso27002.title: Information deletion
iso27002.description: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.

iso27002.title: Data masking
iso27002.description: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organisation's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration.

iso27002.title: Data leakage prevention
iso27002.description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.

iso27002.title: Information backup
iso27002.description: Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.

iso27002.title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
iso27002.description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

iso27002.title: Logging
iso27002.description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.

iso27002.title: Monitoring activities
iso27002.description: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

iso27002.title: Clock synchronisation
iso27002.description: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organisation shall be synchronised to approved time sources.

iso27002.title: Use of privileged utility programs
iso27002.description: The use of utility programs that can be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

iso27002.title: Installation of software on operational systems
iso27002.description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems.

iso27002.title: Networks security
iso27002.description: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

iso27002.title: Security of network services
iso27002.description: Security mechanisms, service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.

iso27002.title: Segregation of networks
iso27002.description: Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated in the organisation's networks.

iso27002.title: Web filtering
iso27002.description: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

iso27002.title: Use of cryptography
iso27002.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

iso27002.title: Secure development life cycle
iso27002.description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

iso27002.title: Application security requirements
iso27002.description: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.

iso27002.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
iso27002.description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system development or integration activities.

iso27002.title: Secure coding
iso27002.description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

iso27002.title: Security testing in development and acceptance
iso27002.description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.

iso27002.title: Outsourced development
iso27002.description: The organisation shall direct, monitor and review the activities related to outsourced system development.

iso27002.title: Separation of development, test and production environments
iso27002.description: Development, testing and production environments shall be separated and secured.

iso27002.title: Change management
iso27002.description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.

iso27002.title: Test information
iso27002.description: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed.

iso27002.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
iso27002.description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.

iso27005.title: Information security risk management
iso27005.description: Establish and maintain a structured information security risk management process integrated with the ISMS and aligned with ISO 31000.

iso27005.title: Context establishment
iso27005.description: Define the internal and external context for risk management, including organisational objectives, stakeholders, risk criteria and scope.

iso27005.title: Information security risk assessment
iso27005.description: Systematically identify, analyse and evaluate information security risks using either the event-based or asset-based approach.

iso27005.title: Risk identification
iso27005.description: Identify information security risks by determining risk sources, events, causes and potential consequences in relation to business objectives.

iso27005.title: Risk analysis
iso27005.description: Analyse identified risks to determine the likelihood and consequences of each risk scenario, considering existing controls.

iso27005.title: Risk evaluation
iso27005.description: Compare risk analysis results with risk criteria to prioritise risks and determine which require treatment.

iso27005.title: Information security risk treatment
iso27005.description: Select and implement appropriate risk treatment options: risk modification, risk retention, risk avoidance or risk sharing.

iso27005.title: Risk treatment option selection
iso27005.description: Select risk treatment options considering risk evaluation results, treatment costs and benefits, and available controls from ISO/IEC 27002.

iso27005.title: Risk treatment plan
iso27005.description: Develop a risk treatment plan specifying selected controls, responsible parties, required resources, timelines and residual risk acceptance.

iso27005.title: Monitoring and review
iso27005.description: Monitor and review the risk management process, its results and the environment to ensure risks are managed effectively and improvements are identified.

iso27005.title: Communication and consultation
iso27005.description: Communicate risk information to stakeholders throughout the risk management process and consult with them on decisions affecting risk.

iso27005.title: Recording and reporting
iso27005.description: Document and report risk management activities, results and decisions to support accountability, continual improvement and audit trails.

iso27017.title: Shared roles and responsibilities within a cloud computing environment
iso27017.description: Roles and responsibilities of cloud service providers and customers for information security shall be documented and communicated.

iso27017.title: Removal of cloud service customer assets
iso27017.description: Procedures for the return, removal or destruction of CSC assets upon contract termination shall be established and agreed.

iso27017.title: Segregation in virtual computing environments
iso27017.description: Virtual computing environments shall be segregated to prevent unauthorised access between CSC environments and between CSP management and CSC environments.

iso27017.title: Virtual machine hardening
iso27017.description: Virtual machine images shall be hardened to meet security requirements and maintained in a secure state throughout their lifecycle.

iso27017.title: Administrator's operational security
iso27017.description: Cloud service administrator activities shall be monitored and logged with procedures defining acceptable use of privileged accounts.

iso27017.title: Monitoring of cloud services
iso27017.description: Cloud service providers shall make monitoring information available to customers and enable CSCs to monitor the cloud services they use.

iso27017.title: Alignment of security management for virtual and physical networks
iso27017.description: The network security management controls applicable to physical networks shall be applied equally to virtual network environments in cloud services.

iso27017.title: Data portability and protection of PII upon contract termination
iso27017.description: CSPs shall provide mechanisms for CSCs to retrieve and securely delete their data upon termination, and certify deletion.

iso27017.title: Information security roles and responsibilities (cloud-specific)
iso27017.description: Cloud-specific information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined, allocated and documented for both provider and customer.

iso27017.title: Operational procedures and responsibilities (cloud)
iso27017.description: Documented operational procedures for cloud services shall define change management, capacity management and separation of development and operational environments.

iso27018.title: Consent and choice
iso27018.description: CSPs shall obtain and document consent before processing PII for any purpose other than the original stated purpose, and provide mechanisms for PII principals to withdraw consent.

iso27018.title: Purpose legitimacy and specification
iso27018.description: PII shall only be processed for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner incompatible with those purposes.

iso27018.title: Collection limitation
iso27018.description: PII collected shall be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary for the purposes for which it is processed.

iso27018.title: Data minimisation
iso27018.description: CSPs shall not use PII processed on behalf of cloud service customers for their own business purposes, including marketing and advertising.

iso27018.title: Use, retention and disclosure limitation
iso27018.description: PII shall not be retained longer than necessary and shall not be disclosed to third parties except as instructed by the CSC or required by law.

iso27018.title: Accuracy and quality
iso27018.description: CSPs shall implement controls to ensure PII is accurate and kept up to date; mechanisms for PII principals to correct inaccuracies shall be provided.

iso27018.title: Openness, transparency and notice
iso27018.description: CSPs shall provide clear information to PII principals about the processing of their PII, including purposes, types of PII processed and retention periods.

iso27018.title: Individual participation and access
iso27018.description: Processes shall be in place to address requests from PII principals for access to, correction or deletion of their PII within a defined timeframe.

iso27018.title: Accountability
iso27018.description: CSPs shall document their privacy responsibilities and be able to demonstrate compliance with applicable privacy principles and requirements.

iso27018.title: Information security
iso27018.description: CSPs shall implement technical and organisational measures to protect PII against loss, unauthorised access, disclosure, alteration and destruction.

iso27018.title: Privacy compliance
iso27018.description: CSPs shall identify and comply with applicable privacy laws, regulations and contractual requirements in all jurisdictions where PII is processed.

iso27701.title: Understanding the organisation and its context (PIMS)
iso27701.description: Extend the ISMS context analysis to include privacy legislation, regulatory requirements, and stakeholder privacy expectations relevant to PII processing.

iso27701.title: Leadership and commitment (PIMS)
iso27701.description: Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment to the PIMS by integrating privacy requirements into organisational strategy and ensuring resources are available.

iso27701.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities (PIMS)
iso27701.description: Consider PII-related risks and opportunities when planning the ISMS, including risks from PII processing activities and privacy-related legal obligations.

iso27701.title: Competence (privacy)
iso27701.description: Ensure persons involved in PII processing have the necessary competence in privacy requirements, data subject rights and applicable regulations.

iso27701.title: Awareness (privacy)
iso27701.description: Persons working under the organisation's authority shall be aware of the PIMS privacy policy, their contribution to PII protection and the implications of non-conformance.

iso27701.title: Identify and document purpose (controller)
iso27701.description: PII controllers shall identify and document the specific purpose(s) for which PII is collected and processed, prior to collection.

iso27701.title: Identify lawful basis (controller)
iso27701.description: Identify, document and maintain the lawful basis for processing PII for each identified purpose, including consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task or legitimate interests.

iso27701.title: Privacy notices to PII principals
iso27701.description: Provide PII principals with clear, transparent notices about the processing of their PII at or before the time of collection.

iso27701.title: Obligations to PII principals (access and correction)
iso27701.description: Implement processes to respond to PII principals' rights including access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, portability and objection.

iso27701.title: Customer agreement (processor)
iso27701.description: PII processors shall only process PII according to documented instructions from the PII controller, established in a legally binding contract.

iso27701.title: Temporary files (processor)
iso27701.description: PII processors shall ensure temporary files and working documents created during PII processing are identified, secured and deleted when no longer required.

iso27701.title: Privacy by design and default (processor)
iso27701.description: PII processors shall implement privacy by design and default principles, ensuring only PII necessary for the specific purpose is processed by default.

bait.title: IT Strategy
bait.description: Institutions must develop and maintain a documented IT strategy that is consistent with the overall business strategy and approved by senior management. The IT strategy must cover objectives, priorities and risk appetite for IT.

bait.title: IT Governance
bait.description: Appropriate IT governance structures must be established including defined roles and responsibilities, committees, an independent Information Security Officer (ISB) and IT risk manager reporting to senior management.

bait.title: Information Risk Management
bait.description: A systematic process for identifying, assessing, treating and monitoring information risks must be implemented, with regular reporting to senior management and integration with the ISMS.

bait.title: Information Security Management
bait.description: An information security management system (ISMS) must be established and maintained, including security policies, standards and guidelines covering all IT systems and data.

bait.title: IT Operations and Availability
bait.description: IT operations must ensure service availability through capacity management, monitoring, incident management, change management and configuration management aligned with business requirements.

bait.title: IT Projects and Applications
bait.description: IT projects must follow a defined project methodology with security requirements integrated from inception. Applications must undergo security assessments before deployment.

bait.title: IT Outsourcing and Third-Party Management
bait.description: IT outsourcing arrangements must be governed by written contracts covering security requirements, audit rights, incident notification and exit management, aligned with EBA Outsourcing Guidelines.

bait.title: Critical Infrastructure Protection (KRITIS)
bait.description: Institutions operating critical infrastructure under KRITIS must implement additional security measures including attack detection systems and biennial proof-of-compliance submissions to BaFin/BSI.

bait.title: Payment Transactions Security
bait.description: Specific security controls must be implemented for payment transaction systems, including fraud detection, strong customer authentication and compliance with PSD2 security requirements.

bait.title: Network and System Security
bait.description: Network security architecture must provide appropriate segmentation, access controls, and perimeter protection. Systems must be hardened and patch management must be systematic.

bait.title: Emergency Management and Business Continuity
bait.description: IT emergency management and business continuity plans must be documented, tested and maintained to ensure recovery of critical IT systems within defined recovery time objectives.

bait.title: IT Audit
bait.description: IT-related audit activities must be conducted by qualified internal auditors or external experts, covering all BAIT requirement areas on a risk-based audit schedule.

pci-dss.title: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
pci-dss.description: Network security controls (NSCs) such as firewalls must be installed and maintained to protect the cardholder data environment (CDE) from untrusted networks. Configuration standards must be documented and reviewed at least every six months.
pci-dss.category: Build and Maintain a Secure Network

pci-dss.title: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
pci-dss.description: All system components must be protected from known weaknesses by following secure configuration standards. Default passwords must be changed, unnecessary services disabled and configuration documentation maintained.
pci-dss.category: Build and Maintain a Secure Network

pci-dss.title: Protect Stored Account Data
pci-dss.description: Protection methods such as encryption, truncation, masking and hashing must be applied to stored account data (PAN, SAD). Primary account numbers must not be stored unless absolutely necessary.

pci-dss.title: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
pci-dss.description: Strong cryptography (e.g. TLS 1.2+) must be used to protect cardholder data during transmission over open public networks. Trusted certificates and protocols must be implemented.

pci-dss.title: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
pci-dss.description: Anti-malware software must be deployed on all system components susceptible to malware. Solutions must be kept current, perform periodic scans and generate audit logs.

pci-dss.title: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
pci-dss.description: Security vulnerabilities must be identified and addressed through patch management. Bespoke and custom software must follow a secure software development lifecycle (SSDLC) including code review and security testing.

pci-dss.title: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
pci-dss.description: Access to system components and cardholder data must be restricted to only those individuals whose job requires such access, enforced through access control systems with deny-all default.

pci-dss.title: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
pci-dss.description: All user accounts must be uniquely identified. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) must be used for all access to the CDE. Password/passphrase requirements must meet defined complexity and rotation standards.

pci-dss.title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
pci-dss.description: Physical access to cardholder data and systems that store, process or transmit cardholder data must be restricted and monitored. Visitor access must be controlled and media containing cardholder data secured.

pci-dss.title: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
pci-dss.description: Audit logs must capture all access to network resources and cardholder data. Logs must be protected from modification, retained for at least 12 months with 3 months immediately available, and reviewed daily.

pci-dss.title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
pci-dss.description: Security vulnerabilities must be discovered and addressed through regular internal and external vulnerability scans (at least quarterly), penetration testing (at least annually) and intrusion detection/prevention systems.

pci-dss.title: Support Information Security with Organisational Policies and Programs
pci-dss.description: An information security policy covering all PCI DSS requirements must be established, published and maintained. Security awareness training, incident response planning and service provider management must be implemented.

tisax.title: Information Security Policy and Organisation
tisax.description: An information security policy approved by management must be in place. Roles, responsibilities and governance structures for information security must be defined, including an appointed Information Security Officer.

tisax.title: Risk Management
tisax.description: A systematic risk management process must identify, assess and treat information security risks. Risk assessments must be reviewed regularly and results documented.

tisax.title: Asset Management
tisax.description: All information assets must be inventoried, classified and assigned to responsible owners. Classification must consider confidentiality, integrity and availability requirements.

tisax.title: Human Resources Security
tisax.description: Security screening, awareness training and clear security responsibilities must be in place for all employees. Exit procedures must ensure removal of access rights upon departure.

tisax.title: Physical and Environmental Security
tisax.description: Physical security perimeters and controls must protect information processing facilities. Access to secure areas must be restricted, logged and monitored. Prototype and development areas require heightened physical protection.

tisax.title: Identity and Access Management
tisax.description: User access must be managed based on least-privilege principles. Strong authentication must be enforced for systems containing sensitive automotive data. Privileged access must be strictly controlled and logged.

tisax.title: Cryptography and Key Management
tisax.description: Cryptographic controls must be applied to protect sensitive information in storage and transmission. A cryptography policy covering algorithm standards and key management must be maintained.

tisax.title: Operations Security
tisax.description: IT operations must ensure change management, vulnerability management, malware protection and secure backup of sensitive automotive information including design data and prototype information.

tisax.title: Incident Management
tisax.description: An incident management process must detect, report, assess and respond to information security incidents involving sensitive automotive data, with documented lessons-learned and root-cause analysis.

tisax.title: Supplier and Third-Party Management
tisax.description: Information security requirements must be contractually imposed on suppliers and third parties that access, process or store sensitive automotive data. Compliance must be regularly assessed.

tisax.title: Prototype Protection — Physical Security
tisax.description: Prototypes, pre-production vehicles and components must be physically protected against unauthorised access, photography and disclosure. Physical access controls must be heightened during development phases.

tisax.title: Prototype Protection — Data Security
tisax.description: Digital data related to prototypes and pre-production development must be classified at the highest protection level and subject to strict access controls, encryption and secure transfer procedures.

cobit.title: Ensure Governance Framework Setting and Maintenance
cobit.description: Analyse and articulate the requirements for the governance of enterprise IT. Put in place and maintain effective enabling structures, principles, processes and practices, with clarity of responsibilities and authority.

cobit.title: Ensure Benefits Delivery
cobit.description: Secure optimal value from IT-enabled initiatives, services and assets; cost-efficient delivery of solutions and services; and a reliable, accurate picture of costs and likely benefits.

cobit.title: Ensure Risk Optimisation
cobit.description: Ensure that IT-related enterprise risk does not exceed the risk appetite and risk tolerance, and that the impact of IT risk to enterprise value is identified and managed.

cobit.title: Ensure Resource Optimisation
cobit.description: Ensure that adequate and sufficient IT-related capabilities (people, process and technology) are available to support enterprise objectives effectively and at optimal cost.

cobit.title: Ensure Stakeholder Engagement
cobit.description: Ensure that enterprise IT stakeholder engagement and communication is effective, and that stakeholders are identified and their requirements, expectations and options understood.

cobit.title: Manage the IT Management Framework
cobit.description: Clarify and maintain the mission and vision of enterprise IT, and implement and maintain mechanisms and authorities for managing IT in a structured way.

cobit.title: Manage Strategy
cobit.description: Provide a holistic view of the current IT environment, the future direction, and the initiatives required to migrate to the desired future environment. Leverage enterprise architecture building blocks.

cobit.title: Manage Enterprise Architecture
cobit.description: Establish a common architecture comprising the business process, information, data, application and technology architecture layers to realise enterprise and IT strategies.

cobit.title: Manage Innovation
cobit.description: Maintain awareness of IT and related service trends, identify innovation opportunities, and plan how to benefit from innovation in relation to business needs.

cobit.title: Manage Portfolio
cobit.description: Execute the strategic direction set for investments, and deliver services and assets as required by the IT strategy. Manage all investments from initiation to value realisation.

cobit.title: Manage Budget and Costs
cobit.description: Manage IT-related financial activities in both business and IT functions, covering budget, cost and benefit management, prioritisation of spending through cost analysis.

cobit.title: Manage Human Resources
cobit.description: Provide a structured approach to ensure optimal structuring, placement, decision rights and skills of human resources. This includes communicating defined roles and responsibilities.

cobit.title: Manage Relationships
cobit.description: Manage the relationship between IT and business in a formal and transparent way that ensures a focus on achieving a common and shared goal.

cobit.title: Manage Service Agreements
cobit.description: Align IT-enabled services and service levels with enterprise needs and expectations, including identification, specification, design, publishing, agreement, and monitoring of IT services.

cobit.title: Manage Vendors
cobit.description: Manage IT-related services provided by all types of vendors to meet enterprise requirements, including selection, management, control and risk assessment of vendors.

cobit.title: Manage Quality
cobit.description: Define and communicate quality requirements in all processes, procedures and related enterprise outcomes. Enable controls, ongoing monitoring, and the use of proven practices for improvement.

cobit.title: Manage Risk
cobit.description: Identify, assess and reduce IT-related risk continually, within tolerance levels set by enterprise executive management and the board.

cobit.title: Manage Security
cobit.description: Define, operate and monitor a system for information security management aligned with ISO/IEC 27001. Ensure security policies, controls, processes and awareness are in place.

cobit.title: Manage Data
cobit.description: Ensure that structured and unstructured data supporting enterprise decisions and services are managed according to data governance requirements for quality, confidentiality, availability and retention.

cobit.title: Manage Programs
cobit.description: Manage all programs in the IT investment portfolio in alignment with the enterprise strategy. Initiate, plan, control and execute programs, and monitor expected benefits.

cobit.title: Manage Requirements Definition
cobit.description: Identify solutions and analyse requirements before acquisition or creation to ensure that they are in line with enterprise strategic requirements. Partner with interested parties to define requirements.

cobit.title: Manage Solutions Identification and Build
cobit.description: Establish and maintain identified solutions in line with enterprise requirements covering design, development, procurement/sourcing and partnering with suppliers/vendors.

cobit.title: Manage Availability and Capacity
cobit.description: Balance current and future needs for availability, performance and capacity with cost-effective service provision. Include assessment of current capabilities, forecasting and risk mitigation.

cobit.title: Manage Organisational Change Enablement
cobit.description: Maximise the likelihood of successfully implementing enterprise-wide change by preparing and committing stakeholders for business and operational change. Address human factors in change management.

cobit.title: Manage IT Changes
cobit.description: Manage all changes to IT infrastructure and applications in a controlled manner, including standard changes and maintenance, to minimise risk to production systems.

cobit.title: Manage IT Change Acceptance and Transitioning
cobit.description: Accept and make operational new solutions, including implementation planning, system and data migration, UAT, communication, training and post-implementation review.

cobit.title: Manage Knowledge
cobit.description: Maintain the availability of relevant, current, validated and reliable knowledge to support all process activities and to facilitate decision making.

cobit.title: Manage Assets
cobit.description: Manage IT assets through their lifecycle to make sure their use delivers value at optimal cost, and that they are accounted for and physically protected.

cobit.title: Manage Configuration
cobit.description: Define and maintain descriptions and relationships between key resources and capabilities required to deliver IT-enabled services. Include collecting CI information, establishing baselines and verifying configuration.

cobit.title: Manage Projects
cobit.description: Manage all IT-enabled investment projects from initiation to closure, with clearly defined project scope, objectives, risks and authority to control project resources.

cobit.title: Manage Operations
cobit.description: Coordinate and execute the activities and operational procedures required to deliver internal and outsourced IT services, including execution of predefined standard procedures and required monitoring activities.

cobit.title: Manage Service Requests and Incidents
cobit.description: Provide timely and effective response to user requests and resolution of all types of incidents. Restore normal service; record and fulfil user requests; record, investigate, diagnose and resolve incidents.

cobit.title: Manage Problems
cobit.description: Identify and classify problems and their root causes, and provide timely resolution to prevent recurring incidents. Provide recommendations for improvements to prevent future occurrences.

cobit.title: Manage Continuity
cobit.description: Establish and maintain a plan to enable business and IT to respond to incidents and disruptions in order to continue operation of critical business processes and required IT services.

cobit.title: Manage Security Services
cobit.description: Protect enterprise information to maintain the level of information security risk acceptable to the enterprise in accordance with the security policy. Establish and maintain information security roles and access privileges.

cobit.title: Manage Business Process Controls
cobit.description: Define and maintain appropriate business process controls to ensure that information related to and processed by in-house or outsourced business processes satisfies all relevant information control requirements.

cobit.title: Manage Performance and Conformance Monitoring
cobit.description: Collect, validate and evaluate business, IT and process goals and metrics. Monitor that processes are performing against agreed performance and conformance goals and metrics.

cobit.title: Manage System of Internal Control
cobit.description: Continuously monitor and evaluate the control environment, including self-assessments and independent assurance reviews. Enable management to identify control deficiencies and initiate improvement actions.

cobit.title: Manage Compliance with External Requirements
cobit.description: Evaluate that IT and business processes are compliant with laws, regulations and contractual requirements. Obtain assurance that requirements have been identified and compliance achieved.

cobit.title: Manage Assurance
cobit.description: Obtain assurance over the completeness and effectiveness of controls supporting enterprise objectives and risk management, by providing independent assurance services to the board and executive management.

iso38500.title: Responsibility
iso38500.description: Individuals and groups within the organisation understand and accept their responsibilities in respect of both supply of, and demand for, IT. Those with responsibility for actions also have the authority to perform those actions.

iso38500.title: Strategy
iso38500.description: The organisation's business strategy takes into account the current and future capabilities of IT; the strategic plans for IT satisfy the current and ongoing needs of the organisation's business strategy.

iso38500.title: Acquisition
iso38500.description: IT acquisitions are made for valid reasons, on the basis of appropriate and ongoing analysis, with clear and transparent decision making. There is an appropriate balance between benefits, opportunities, costs and risks.

iso38500.title: Performance
iso38500.description: IT is fit for purpose in supporting the organisation, providing the services, levels of service and service quality required to meet current and future business requirements.

iso38500.title: Conformance
iso38500.description: IT complies with all mandatory legislation and regulations. Policies and practices are clearly defined, implemented and enforced.

iso38500.title: Human Behaviour
iso38500.description: IT policies, practices and decisions demonstrate respect for human behaviour, including the current and evolving needs of all the people in the process.

iso38500.title: Evaluate (Governing Body Task)
iso38500.description: The governing body shall evaluate current and future use of IT by examining and judging the strategy, proposals and supply arrangements for IT, to ensure that IT meets business needs.

iso38500.title: Direct (Governing Body Task)
iso38500.description: The governing body shall direct preparation and implementation of plans and policies, allocate responsibilities and direct management to implement the plans and policies.

iso38500.title: Monitor (Governing Body Task)
iso38500.description: The governing body shall monitor conformance to policies, performance against plans, and ensure that IT performs in accordance with the agreed requirements.

iso31000.title: Principles
iso31000.description: Risk management shall be integrated, structured, comprehensive, customised, inclusive, dynamic, based on best available information, and account for human and cultural factors, with a commitment to continual improvement.

iso31000.title: Framework
iso31000.description: Design, implement, evaluate and improve a risk management framework through which the organisation integrates risk management into all significant activities and functions.

iso31000.title: Leadership and commitment
iso31000.description: Top management and oversight bodies shall ensure risk management is integrated into organisational activities through policy, mandate, authority, resources and accountability arrangements.

iso31000.title: Integration
iso31000.description: Integration of risk management throughout the organisation requires understanding of structures, contexts and objectives at each level; risk management is not a stand-alone activity.

iso31000.title: Design
iso31000.description: Design the risk management framework by understanding the organisation's context, articulating risk management commitment and assigning organisational roles, authorities and responsibilities.

iso31000.title: Implementation
iso31000.description: Implement the risk management framework and process by developing an appropriate plan, identifying decision-making points and incorporating risk management into all organisational practices.

iso31000.title: Evaluation
iso31000.description: Periodically measure risk management framework performance against its purpose, implementation plans and behaviour, and determine whether it remains suitable to support achieving the organisation's objectives.

iso31000.title: Improvement
iso31000.description: Continually monitor and adapt the risk management framework to address changes in external and internal context, identify improvements and report these to oversight bodies.

iso31000.title: Risk management process — general
iso31000.description: The risk management process involves the systematic application of policies, procedures and practices to communication, consultation, establishing context, and assessing, treating, monitoring, reviewing, recording and reporting risk.

iso31000.title: Communication and consultation
iso31000.description: The purpose of communication and consultation is to assist relevant stakeholders in understanding risk, the basis on which decisions are made and the reasons why particular actions are required.

iso31000.title: Scope, context and criteria
iso31000.description: Customise the risk management process by defining the scope, internal and external context, risk criteria (including risk appetite, risk tolerance and risk acceptance levels) and performance criteria.

iso31000.title: Risk assessment
iso31000.description: Risk assessment is the overall process of risk identification, risk analysis and risk evaluation. It provides an understanding of risks and forms the basis for risk treatment decisions.

iso31000.title: Risk identification
iso31000.description: Identify sources of risk, areas of impact, events and their causes and potential consequences. The goal is to generate a comprehensive list of risks based on events that might create, enhance, prevent, degrade, accelerate or delay objectives.

iso31000.title: Risk analysis
iso31000.description: Analyse risks by understanding their nature, sources, consequences and likelihood. Consider the effectiveness of existing controls, confidence in data, relationships between risks and their dependencies.

iso31000.title: Risk evaluation
iso31000.description: Compare the results of risk analysis with risk criteria to determine where additional action is required. Decisions should take into account the wider context, including the tolerance of the risk.

iso31000.title: Risk treatment
iso31000.description: Select and implement options for addressing risk: avoid, take or increase risk to pursue an opportunity; remove the risk source; change likelihood or consequences; share the risk; or retain the risk.

iso31000.title: Monitoring and review
iso31000.description: The purpose of monitoring and review is to assure and improve the quality and effectiveness of process design, implementation and outcomes. Continual monitoring and periodic reviews are essential.

iso31000.title: Recording and reporting
iso31000.description: The risk management process and its outcomes should be documented and reported through appropriate mechanisms, satisfying accountability needs and decision-making at all organisational levels.

itil-v3.title: Strategy Management for IT Services
itil-v3.description: Define and maintain the IT service provider's perspective, position, plans and patterns with respect to its services and the management of those services.

itil-v3.title: Service Portfolio Management
itil-v3.description: Ensure the service provider has the right mix of services to meet required business outcomes at an appropriate level of investment. Manage the pipeline, catalogue and retired services.

itil-v3.title: Financial Management for IT Services
itil-v3.description: Secure appropriate level of funding to design, develop and deliver services that meet the strategy of the organisation, while balancing costs and ensuring value.

itil-v3.title: Demand Management
itil-v3.description: Understand, anticipate and influence customer demand for services and the provision of capacity to meet these demands.

itil-v3.title: Business Relationship Management
itil-v3.description: Establish and maintain a business relationship between the service provider and the customer, based on understanding the customer's business and needs.

itil-v3.title: Service Level Management
itil-v3.description: Negotiate, agree and document appropriate IT service targets with the business, and then monitor and produce reports on actual delivery against agreed service levels.

itil-v3.title: Availability Management
itil-v3.description: Define, analyse, plan, measure and improve all aspects of the availability of IT services to ensure that the level of availability delivered matches the agreed service levels.

itil-v3.title: Capacity Management
itil-v3.description: Ensure that the capacity of IT services and IT infrastructure is able to deliver agreed service level targets in a cost effective and timely manner.

itil-v3.title: IT Service Continuity Management
itil-v3.description: Manage risks that could seriously affect IT services. Ensure the IT service provider can always provide minimum agreed service levels, reducing risk to acceptable levels.

itil-v3.title: Information Security Management
itil-v3.description: Align IT security with business security and ensure confidentiality, integrity and availability of assets, information, data and IT services.

itil-v3.title: Supplier Management
itil-v3.description: Obtain value for money from suppliers and ensure underpinning contracts and agreements are aligned with business needs. Manage relationships with suppliers.

itil-v3.title: Design Coordination
itil-v3.description: Coordinate all service design activities, processes and resources. Produce quality, consistent service design packages to be handed over to service transition.

itil-v3.title: Change Management
itil-v3.description: Control the lifecycle of all changes, enabling beneficial changes to be made with minimum disruption to IT services.

itil-v3.title: Service Asset and Configuration Management
itil-v3.description: Ensure assets required to deliver services are properly controlled and accurate, reliable information about assets is available when and where needed.

itil-v3.title: Release and Deployment Management
itil-v3.description: Plan, schedule and control build, test and deployment of releases. Deliver new functionality required by the business while protecting the integrity of existing services.

itil-v3.title: Knowledge Management
itil-v3.description: Improve the quality of management decision making by ensuring that reliable, secure information is available throughout the service lifecycle.

itil-v3.title: Service Evaluation and Validation Testing
itil-v3.description: Provide objective evidence that the new or changed service supports the business and its requirements, including agreed utility and warranty.

itil-v3.title: Incident Management
itil-v3.description: Manage the lifecycle of all incidents to restore normal service operation as quickly as possible and minimise the adverse impact on business operations.

itil-v3.title: Problem Management
itil-v3.description: Manage the lifecycle of all problems from first identification through further investigation and documentation to ultimate closure. Identify root causes and prevent recurrence.

itil-v3.title: Event Management
itil-v3.description: Manage events throughout their lifecycle. Events include any detectable or discernible occurrence that has significance for the management of IT infrastructure.

itil-v3.title: Request Fulfilment
itil-v3.description: Manage the lifecycle of service requests from users. Provide a channel for users to request and receive standard services, and provide information to users about services available.

itil-v3.title: Access Management
itil-v3.description: Grant authorised users the right to use a service while preventing access to non-authorised users. Execute policies and actions defined in information security management.

itil-v3.title: Seven-Step Improvement Process
itil-v3.description: Identify and implement improvements to IT services and service management. Define what should be measured, gather and process data, analyse and present information, and implement improvement actions.

itil-v3.title: Service Measurement and Reporting
itil-v3.description: Define, collect and report on service performance metrics to provide the business with an accurate picture of service performance and to support continual service improvement.

itil-v4.title: Architecture Management
itil-v4.description: Provide an understanding of all the different elements that make up an organisation and how those elements interrelate, enabling the organisation to effectively achieve its current and future objectives.

itil-v4.title: Continual Improvement
itil-v4.description: Align the organisation's practices and services with changing business needs through the ongoing identification and improvement of services, service components, practices, or any element involved in the efficient and effective management of products and services.

itil-v4.title: Information Security Management
itil-v4.description: Protect the information needed by the organisation to conduct its business. This includes understanding and managing risks to confidentiality, integrity and availability of information.

itil-v4.title: Knowledge Management
itil-v4.description: Maintain and improve the effective, efficient, and convenient use of information and knowledge across the organisation.

itil-v4.title: Measurement and Reporting
itil-v4.description: Support good decision-making and continual improvement by decreasing the levels of uncertainty. Ensure relevant measurements are defined and data is collected to provide meaningful insights.

itil-v4.title: Organisational Change Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure that changes in an organisation are smoothly and successfully implemented and that lasting benefits are achieved by managing the human aspects of the changes.

itil-v4.title: Portfolio Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure the organisation has the right mix of programmes, projects, products and services to execute its strategy within its funding and resource constraints.

itil-v4.title: Project Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure all projects in the organisation are successfully delivered. Achieve agreed outcomes and manage risks in a structured way.

itil-v4.title: Relationship Management
itil-v4.description: Establish and nurture the links between the organisation and its stakeholders at strategic and tactical levels. Identify, analyse and monitor relationships.

itil-v4.title: Risk Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure that the organisation understands and effectively handles risks. Risk management preserves and increases value through managing uncertainty.

itil-v4.title: Service Financial Management
itil-v4.description: Support the organisation's strategies and plans for service management by ensuring that the organisation's financial resources and investments are being used effectively.

itil-v4.title: Strategy Management
itil-v4.description: Formulate the goals of the organisation and adopt the courses of action and allocation of resources necessary for achieving those goals.

itil-v4.title: Supplier Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure that the organisation's suppliers and their performances are managed appropriately to support the seamless provision of quality products and services.

itil-v4.title: Workforce and Talent Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure the organisation has the right people with the appropriate skills and knowledge and in the correct roles to support its business objectives.

itil-v4.title: Availability Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure services deliver agreed levels of availability to meet the needs of customers and users. Includes planning, monitoring and reviewing availability against requirements.

itil-v4.title: Business Analysis
itil-v4.description: Analyse a business or some element of it, define its associated needs, and recommend solutions to address these needs and/or solve a business problem.

itil-v4.title: Capacity and Performance Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure that services achieve agreed and expected performance levels, satisfying current and future demand in a cost-effective way.

itil-v4.title: Change Enablement
itil-v4.description: Maximise the number of successful IT and product changes by ensuring that risks have been properly assessed, authorising changes to proceed and managing a change schedule.

itil-v4.title: Incident Management
itil-v4.description: Minimise the negative impact of incidents by restoring normal service operation as quickly as possible. Includes logging, classifying, prioritising and resolving incidents.

itil-v4.title: IT Asset Management
itil-v4.description: Plan and manage the full lifecycle of all IT assets, to help the organisation: maximise value, control costs, manage risks, support decision-making about purchase, re-use, retirement and disposal.

itil-v4.title: Monitoring and Event Management
itil-v4.description: Systematically observe services and service components, and record and report selected changes of state identified as events. Identify and prioritise infrastructure, services, business processes and information security events.

itil-v4.title: Problem Management
itil-v4.description: Reduce the likelihood and impact of incidents by identifying actual and potential causes of incidents, and managing workarounds and known errors.

itil-v4.title: Release Management
itil-v4.description: Make new and changed services and features available for use. Includes managing the release package, scheduling and deploying releases.

itil-v4.title: Service Configuration Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure that accurate and reliable information about the configuration of services, and the CIs that support them, is available when and where needed.

itil-v4.title: Service Continuity Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure availability and performance of a service are maintained at sufficient levels in case of a disaster. Provide a framework for building organisational resilience.

itil-v4.title: Service Design
itil-v4.description: Design products and services that are fit for purpose and fit for use, and that can be delivered by the organisation and its ecosystem of partners and suppliers.

itil-v4.title: Service Desk
itil-v4.description: Capture demand for incident resolution and service requests. Provide a clear path for users to report issues, queries and requests, and have them acknowledged, classified, owned and actioned.

itil-v4.title: Service Level Management
itil-v4.description: Set clear business-based targets for service levels, and ensure that delivery of services can be properly assessed, monitored and managed against these targets.

itil-v4.title: Service Request Management
itil-v4.description: Support the agreed quality of a service by handling all pre-defined, user-initiated service requests in an effective and user-friendly manner.

itil-v4.title: Service Validation and Testing
itil-v4.description: Ensure that new or changed products and services meet defined requirements. Establish and maintain defined and agreed service quality.

itil-v4.title: Deployment Management
itil-v4.description: Move new or changed hardware, software, documentation, processes or any other component to live environments. Manage deployments across environments from development to production.

itil-v4.title: Infrastructure and Platform Management
itil-v4.description: Oversee the infrastructure and platforms used by an organisation. This enables the monitoring of technology solutions available, including solutions offered by third parties.

itil-v4.title: Software Development and Management
itil-v4.description: Ensure that applications meet internal and external stakeholder needs in terms of functionality, reliability, maintainability, compliance and auditability.
